posts.name: Kris_P@tten
posts.cooked:
Big loss for the wine world. Great passion in everything he did and will be sorely missed, especially gabbing with him about Whiskey and Bourbon on a personal level.

vinous.com

Vinous | Explore All Things Wine

posts.name: Eric LeVine
posts.cooked: OMG, just saw that. Such a talented and nice guy. Far, FAR too young!!!

posts.cooked: RIP. Huge loss.

posts.cooked: Wow. This is terrible.

posts.name: Adam Altman
posts.cooked: Horribly sad news. Such a funny, personable and knowledgeable guy. The wine world needs more people like him.

posts.name: Brian Tuite  GOML!
posts.cooked: Just opened the email. That sucks. RIP

user_status.description: Wine doesn’t have to be tertiary to be enjoyable

posts.name: Bob Hughes
posts.cooked: I loved his IG posts on “fine dining”. Way too young. RIP, Josh.
Terrible news. He was a friend. So so so sad.

posts.name: Tom DeBiase
posts.cooked:
Awful news, way way too young

Tom

posts.name: Vincent Fritzsche
posts.cooked: Incredibly sad

posts.name: Eric Rogers
posts.cooked: Very sad news. I’ve enjoyed his work for many years.

posts.name: larry schaffer
posts.cooked: Very sad news indeed. He was always very approachable, but also very knowledgeable. My sincerest condolences to his family.

posts.name: Michel Abood
posts.cooked: So sad, my sincerest condolences to his family. He was such a pleasure to talk to when he came to our tastings and at dinners, his wit will be missed.

posts.name: Mar.kus Meer
posts.cooked: Oof… FWIW, perhaps my favorite wine reviewer. Grateful for all of the Rhone & Santa Barbara wines I’ve enjoyed over the years and the many more moments I will be able to celebrate because of his work.

posts.name: Paul Jaouen
posts.cooked: So sad. He was such a down to earth kind of guy and I loved dining with him. I didn’t see much of him since Covid but we did drink some great wines together with friends this past December. RIP.

posts.name: R 1 c k  D y 3 r
posts.cooked: Never met him but I loved his work. He’ll be missed. Condolences to his family and friends.

posts.name: Rich K0rz€nk0
posts.cooked: Rest in Peace Josh, very sad indeed.

posts.name: David K o l i n
posts.cooked: Terrible
posts.display_username: David K o l i n

posts.cooked: Peace to his family and friends.

posts.name: Julian Marshall
posts.cooked: That’s horrible news. How awful. What a loss. When I subscribed to Vinous we had great chats about wine but also music, since he liked much of the same stuff as me. He introduced me to lots of Beaujolais producers I’d never heard of. Seemed like such a nice man. I’m gobsmacked.

posts.name: Gregory Dal Piaz
posts.cooked: Saw him for dinner last month. He was in good spirits and, as usual, could talk endlessly about anything and everything. What a loss, both professionally and personally for those who knew him. RIP.

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked: I am very sad to read about this. I used to buy wine from Josh years ago when he worked for David Schildknecht in DC. RIP.

posts.name: N@th@n V@nd€rgrift
posts.cooked: Plain Old Pearson’s. My dad used to take me with him shopping there. Legendary.

posts.name: N@th@n V@nd€rgrift
posts.cooked: I was jealous when I saw those pictures and made a mental note to make sure to see him next time I was in NY. Damn. A terrible loss for the community.

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked: Yes. I often would order wines from David, talking with him about what I should buy. But, when the wines came in, they would be at a warehouse. I quickly learned that when I asked David to get the wine to the store so I could pick it up on the way home from work, often he would forget and the wine would not be there. I learned to ask Josh to get the wine to the store and he always got it there. He seemed so young in those days (late 1980s and early 1990s). Life passes too fast.

posts.name: Todd Hamina
posts.cooked: The guy was an encyclopedia of all things food and drink. I’ll miss our conversations. Sad day.

posts.name: Marcus Goodfellow
posts.cooked: So sad, Josh was a tremendous reviewer and a great person to spend an afternoon with. As noted already, an absolute encyclopedia of wine, and many other things.

posts.name: Don Cornutt
posts.cooked: Such sad news. Way too young.

posts.name: Doug Lee
posts.cooked: Rest in peace Josh.

posts.name: Hal Blumberg
posts.cooked:
I never had the pleasure of meeting Josh, but hold his work in the highest esteem. Condolences to his family, friends, and many followers.

Cheers,

Hal

posts.name: Eric White
posts.cooked: shocking. RIP Josh

posts.name: Terry H a r r i s
posts.cooked: How terribly sad. RIP

posts.name: Nola.Palomar
posts.cooked: This hurt me so much to hear this. Fuck. Just FUCK. Sorry, R.I.P. Josh, condolences to his wife and daughters.

posts.name: Jason L e f l e r
posts.cooked: i’m speechless. rest in peace.
posts.display_username: Jason L e f l e r

posts.name: Adam Lee
posts.cooked: Josh was incredibly special… https://claricewinecompany.com/on-a-friendship-between-a-wine-writer-and-a-winemaker/

posts.name: Nola.Palomar
posts.cooked:
Adam_Lee:

Josh was incredibly special…

Preach I am just gobsmacked.

posts.name: Brad Baker
posts.cooked: Josh was a treasure. Such a great guy and so humble considering his skill set. He really deserved much more attention and many more accolades than he got, but that never bothered him. I always found him willing to share, teach, have fun, and just plain enjoy life. Amongst critics, his palate is the best I have personally encountered in my lifetime so far. I ache for his family, friends, the wine world, and the world at large. We lost a good one far too soon.

posts.name: Jayson Cohen
posts.cooked:
He was a friend of mine for over twenty years. I just found out and my skull won’t stop tingling in shock. After that dinner in December, he and I went out to a dive bar into the wee hours. I put him into a taxi to Penn Station around 2 in the morning, and that’s the last time I saw him…. Heart broken.

Here he is chatting and laughing with you, Paul, off camera to my left.

image1673×2231 953 KB

posts.name: J a y H a c k
posts.cooked: I only met him once, at a Posner tasting years ago. He, my wife and I all tasted the same two Village Chablis. Josh agreed with Rebecca and disagreed with me as to which was better and why, so he must have had a terrible palate, but I remember him being a very nice guy that we joked with for a while. He was way too young. It always hits harder when someone younger that I dies. Very sad.
posts.display_username: J a y H a c k

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Terrible news. Josh was a great taster and someone I would have loved to have worked with.

posts.name: James Wright
posts.cooked:
very sad news, and a loss to all –

a friend since the late eighties – succeeded me as Schildknecht’s assistant
at a DC retailer

posts.name: Scott Brunson
posts.cooked:
Horrible news.
RIP

posts.name: Dan Kravitz
posts.cooked:
Josh worked for me and my company a long time ago, then went to work for Rosenthal before finding his place as a writer. He had a brilliant mind, and if he could be quite sardonic there was a lot of kindness and thoughtful consideration underneath all of that. He will be missed. RIP.

Dan Kravitz

posts.name: Jorge Henriquez
posts.cooked: Aww man. Sad news indeed. Josh was a good guy through and through. Condolences to his family.

posts.name: Paul Jaouen
posts.cooked: Jayson, thank you for that photo.

posts.name: Bill Mauger
posts.cooked: My good friend in Indiana that got me into wine worked with Josh in DC in the 80’s - nothing but deep respect for his work and talent - so sad! He covered many of the regions I treasure - and i loved his writing.

This thread is a great tribute to such a genuinely nice man.

I bought wine from him at Pearson’s and never knew it was him until reviewing this thread. RIP indeed.

posts.name: Ian Dorin
posts.cooked: This was tough news to hear yesterday. Still doesn’t quite feel real. I could listen to him talk about wine for hours. I remember at a West of West tasting he was super excited about Wayfarer. He told me don’t be a snob, go taste the wines. He was spot on, they were incredible wines. Years later he turned me on to Walter Scott. We were talking about doing a cheesesteak crawl in Philly.

posts.name: Kelly Walker
posts.cooked: So very sad. Always great to be around. Had a story or a joke for every topic. He came to CLT each year to do reviews. Everyone would have smiles on their faces, including mine, whenever he was in the room.

posts.name: Peter Hirdt
posts.cooked: I didn’t know Josh well. Only from what I could glean from maybe three or four dinners years ago. What I remember most was that he was truly funny—witty would be a better description, but in a laugh-out-loud way—how much he loved grilling meat, and most of all how proud he was of his daughters and how dedicated he was to them. Such a sad ending.

posts.name: Mike Silverman
posts.cooked: I will mis him. He was a mench. He brought warmth and pleasure with him everywhere he went.

posts.name: Dave Paige
posts.cooked:
Josh was a genuinely good guy. He could hang out with you in a way that never felt like you were a winemaker trying to impress a wine writer, and did so effortlessly.

He will be greatly missed!

posts.name: D@vid K@ntr0witz
posts.cooked: Just got a mailer from Benchmark “commemorating” Josh and then proceeding to list for sale a bunch of wines reviewed by him. Seems a little…off to me.

posts.name: Ian Dorin
posts.cooked: Funny, I was thinking the same thing. There should have been no solicitation with it.

posts.cooked:

He always struck me as passionate about wine, a straight shooter and all around excellent guy. Such sad news. Too young!

RT
